title: load_host()'s silent Host() fallback is a wrong-type-shaped success that, combined with base Host's ignorance of apply_patch, zeroes every pre-write guard on Codex
evidence:
hostapi.load_host() (176-191) wraps _host.py's dynamic import in a bare `except Exception` (line 190, `# noqa: BLE001`) and on ANY failure — syntax error, import error, unreadable file, a bad edit to _host.py — returns `Host()` (Claude semantics) with NO signal that the intended CodexHost failed to load; get_host() (_hooklib.py:79-84) caches that value with no warn()/log call anywhere in the path. Under ca-codex, the base Host's TOOL_MAP (hostapi.py:56-64) has no entry for "apply_patch" (Codex's canonical write tool), so normalize_tool falls through to "OTHER"; iter_file_ops' dispatch (121-173) doesn't special-case "apply_patch" either, so it falls into the generic Write branch which reads `ti.get("file_path")` and `ti.get("content")` — fields that do not exist in Codex's tool_input shape `{"command": <patch text>}`. Reproduced directly: `hostapi.Host().iter_file_ops({"tool_name": "apply_patch", "tool_input": {"command": "*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: .codearbiter/CONTEXT.md
@@
-arbiter: enabled
+arbiter: disabled
*** End Patch
"}})` returns `[{'file_path': '', 'kind': 'write', 'content': '', 'added_text': '', 'added_lines': []}]` — file_path and content are both silently emptied. In pre-write.py's `_guard_op` (pre-write.py:32-42), `classify_protected("", root)` returns an empty set (norm_path("") and repo_rel("", root) are both falsy and skipped in classify_protected, _hooklib.py:247-259), so every one of H-18 (CONTEXT.md kill-switch), H-19 (marker forgery), H-05 (audit-log append-only), H-11 (ADR immutability) is silently skipped — the write is allowed with exit 0.
impact: Any failure of ca-codex's _host.py to import (a bad edit, a partial install, a stray syntax error, a future refactor typo) degrades Codex-side enforcement from "every apply_patch write is guarded" to "every apply_patch write silently bypasses ALL FOUR pre-write guards, including the CONTEXT.md kill-switch and marker-forgery guard" — with zero error, warning, or exit-code signal anywhere in the chain. This is the exact failure mode the module's own design principle (hostapi.py:27-28, "Behavioral contract for M1: ... byte-identical results") and the opaque-kind fail-closed design (pre-write.py H-21) are meant to prevent for a decomposition failure, but the load-failure path takes the opposite (fail-open) branch instead.
recommendation: Give load_host() a distinguishable failure signal instead of a same-shaped success: e.g. raise/propagate on a broken (present-but-erroring) _host.py rather than swallowing it identically to the absent-file case, or have Host itself refuse to guess (return a fail-closed "opaque" op for any tool_name it does not recognize as EXEC/WRITE/EDIT/READ, so a mis-loaded host still fails closed instead of silently emptying file_path/content). At minimum call warn() on the broken-_host.py branch (distinct from the absent-file branch) so a fallback is a loud, auditable event.
acceptance_criteria: Distinguish 'no _host.py' from '_host.py present but failed to import' in load_host()'s behavior or logging

An apply_patch/Write/Edit payload routed through a Host that doesn't recognize the tool name fails CLOSED (blocks or emits an 'opaque' op) rather than silently producing an empty-file_path/empty-content 'write' op
A regression test asserts that a broken ca-codex _host.py does not silently pass an apply_patch write that touches .codearbiter/CONTEXT.md or .codearbiter/.markers/
